analysis_metadata.paper_title: Observation of a New Particle in the Search for the Standard Model Higgs Boson with the ATLAS Detector at the LHC
analysis_metadata.notes: Analysis design inspired by the ATLAS Higgs discovery diphoton analysis. Implemented on the ATLAS 13 TeV open data release (36 fb-1). Converted/unconverted photon categorization removed due to missing variables in the open dataset.

analysis_objectives.description: Measure the Higgs boson signal strength using a diphoton invariant-mass fit with five analysis categories.
analysis_objectives.special_considerations: Simplified implementation of the original ATLAS discovery diphoton analysis adapted for the open data sample.

signal_signatures.event_topology_description: Two isolated photons forming a narrow resonance near 125 GeV in the diphoton invariant mass spectrum.
signal_signatures.special_considerations: Five-category scheme designed to preserve sensitivity while simplifying the original categorization.

background_processes.special_treatment: Background modeled with analytic functions fitted to the diphoton invariant mass spectrum in each category.
Jets misidentified as photons contribute to the smooth diphoton mass spectrum.
Two jets misidentified as photons contribute to background.
Electrons reconstructed as photons contribute to diphoton candidates.

signal_regions.selection_summary: At least two jets with |eta| < 4.5, dijet invariant mass m_jj > 400 GeV, and pseudorapidity separation delta_eta_jj > 2.8.
signal_regions.optimization_strategy: Topology-based categorization to enhance VBF sensitivity.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Events assigned here are removed from the other categories.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Two photons with |eta| < 0.75 and pTt < 60 GeV.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Converted and unconverted photon categories merged.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Two photons with |eta| < 0.75 and pTt >= 60 GeV.
signal_regions.purpose: Enhance sensitivity to VH and VBF-like production.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Converted and unconverted photon categories merged.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Events not in central or 2-jet category with pTt < 60 GeV.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Converted and unconverted photon categories merged.

signal_regions.selection_summary: Events not in central or 2-jet category with pTt >= 60 GeV.
signal_regions.special_considerations: Converted and unconverted photon categories merged.

control_regions.selection_summary: Control samples used to validate background modeling and photon identification performance.
control_regions.justification: Used to verify the smooth background parameterization used in the signal fit.

fit_setup.fit_purpose: Extract Higgs signal strength from diphoton invariant mass distribution.
fit_setup.differences_between_fit_versions: Original ATLAS discovery analysis used ten categories including photon conversion information; this implementation uses five categories due to limited variables in open data.
fit_setup.special_considerations: Background modeled with analytic functions fitted to data.

results.dominant_uncertainties: photon energy scale, photon identification efficiency, background model uncertainty
results.notes: Expected to observe Higgs resonance near 125 GeV.

analysis_design_insights.description: Categories based on photon eta topology, diphoton pTt, and VBF jet topology.
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Maintain sensitivity while simplifying the discovery analysis categorization.
analysis_design_insights.special_considerations: Conversion variables unavailable in open data.

analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Enhances sensitivity to VBF Higgs production.

analysis_design_insights.description: Smooth analytic background fit to diphoton mass distribution.
analysis_design_insights.reason_for_use: Robust modeling of continuum diphoton background.

sensitivity_drivers.main_limiting_systematics: photon energy scale and background modeling
